event: Flight controller availability from non-Unknown sources will remain critically constrained with 93% of 327 units from unverified origins, creating diversion risk and procurement friction for defense programs through 2027.
drivers: 305 of 327 flight controllers (93%) listed as 'Unknown' origin in parts database
Only 22 Ukraine-origin flight controllers identified as traceable alternatives
Zero price delta on flight controllers despite supply opacity indicates frozen sourcing patterns

event: ESC supply constraint from China will intensify as US defense contractors pivot to NDAA-compliant alternatives, causing 8-12 week lead times for non-Chinese ESCs to extend to 16-20 weeks.
drivers: 71% of ESCs sourced from China per parts database (119/167 units)
NDAA compliance delta increased 2.41% in last snapshot indicating active sourcing shifts
Only 26 US-origin ESCs in database versus 119 Chinese creating severe capacity gap

event: Battery supply chains will regionalize with Taiwan and US suppliers capturing 15-20% additional market share from China as defense contractors derisk procurement, extending lead times for premium cells to 10-14 weeks.
drivers: Current 46% China sourcing (100/221 batteries) versus 46% US creates balanced but vulnerable supply
NDAA delta increase of 2.41% indicates active compliance-driven sourcing changes
Zero battery price delta despite sourcing pressure suggests upcoming repricing event

event: AI accelerator prices will increase 25-40% as defense autonomy software platforms like Teledyne FLIR Prism SKR drive demand for edge compute in FPV and loitering munition applications.
drivers: Teledyne FLIR expanded Prism SKR to unified autonomy platform for FPV drones and loitering munitions announced April 7
Only 14 AI accelerators in parts database with median price $84 indicating nascent market
64% of AI accelerators from US/Israel (11/14) limiting competitive pricing pressure

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Convergence of autonomous targeting software with commercial FPV platforms creating new component demand

event: FPV camera supply from China will face scrutiny and potential export controls as counter-UAS and electronic warfare applications proliferate, causing 15-25% price increases on remaining stock.
drivers: 97% of FPV cameras (187/193) sourced from China per parts database creating single-origin dependency
Teledyne FLIR Prism SKR targeting FPV drones for defense applications per April 7 announcement
Median price of $21 indicates commodity status vulnerable to export control shocks

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Dual-use FPV camera technology becoming embedded in military autonomy platforms

event: C2 datalink systems will experience supply diversification as companies like NT iBuzz (Thailand partnership) enter secure communications market, reducing median lead time from 14 weeks to 10 weeks by Q1 2027.
drivers: NT iBuzz Thailand state-owned subsidiary entering secure UAV communications per April 8 announcement
Only 18 C2 datalink systems in database with 61% US-origin indicating limited vendor base
Median price of $499 suggests commercial pressure for lower-cost alternatives

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Public safety and border security applications driving commercial-grade secure datalink development
